Vision: Explain Nexus and Personal OS as project-development and discovery infrastructure, not miscellaneous personal tooling. Mechanism: Extract creativity, network, learning, imagination, leadership, and project-generation mechanisms. Plan: Rewrite Nexus and planning pages around upstream/downstream project movement. Status: source library identified as candidate scholarly support; direct quotation requires exact passage review. Remaining gap: Need exact project-method and creativity passages. Source pool: 151 deduplicated local title candidates; 154 catalogue support rows. Examples: Creativity Research; The Heritage, Creativity and Innovation Nexus. source library scholarly-source routing with author correction. Raw filenames often append Nik_Murano and are not treated as authorship evidence. Catalogue rows are preferred for authors...
fields.plan: How this briefing says the vision should be accomplished, translated into a public project route. Session start: paste 00_CORE_CHARTER.md + a filled 01_ROOM_PACKET_TEMPLATE.md (Section 0 YAML + evidence packet) — Declare MODE: EXPLORATION for options (no code emitted), INCORPORATION for merge-ready outputs and handoff zip — Supply Minimum Evidence Packet before asking for code — For each room in scope (max 3): produce Phase I spec, Phase II UI scaffold, Phase III data/persistence/tests, integration notes — Consolidate Master Command Registry, Hub Manifest Additions, Master Integration Checklist across rooms — Re-anchor after each room by quoting the charter/evidence (no memory reconstruction). Wire or preserve the implementation files: None — explicitly excluded. "This pack does not include MuranoOS source code or room implementations. It is the governance scaffold for generating them safely."
fields.remaining_gaps: Promote the packet from documented handoff to live implementation only after the declared caveats are resolved: apply or wire the scaffold, run the stated validation or smoke-test sequence, and keep unresolved assumptions visible.
fields.detailed_source_vision: This package is the governance scaffold that controls how every MuranoOS room handoff package in this family is produced. It is a refactor of MURANOOS — ROOM PREPARATION PROTOCOL v3.0 into a smaller, auditable prompt pack split into two work products: a persistent Core Charter (non-negotiable rules) and a per-session Room Execution Packet template. It is explicitly prompt/tooling only — it does not include MuranoOS source code or room implementations; it constrains how those get generated safely. The charter's job is to constrain an LLM assistant to produce incorporation-ready deliverables with strict controls against four failure modes: fabricated codebase details, silent context drift, non-integrable output, cross-room inconsistency. It does this via five linked mechanisms: a mode switch (EXPLORATION vs INCORPORATION), a mandatory provenance tag set with evidence pointers, three confirmation tiers (Tier 0 blocking / Tier 1 deferable / Tier 2 cosmetic), a...
fields.detailed_source_mechanism: , state the backend/frontend or packet components, and decide whether the handoff is ready for engineering, public explanation, or archival retention

maintenance_rule: Update this blueprint before strengthening public claims on the website. Do not use word count, module count, or status percentage as a substitute for concrete project substance.
claim_ceiling_rule: Public claims may rise only when the built components, source use, status, and remaining gaps are updated here and reflected in the rendered page.

public_standard_check.Vision: This document supports the public account of Blueprint by preserving the project purpose, reader-facing claim, and professional-portfolio role.
public_standard_check.Mechanism: It connects source material, project components, implementation state, and next actions so the work can be inspected rather than merely asserted.
public_standard_check.What is built: The supporting evidence is the named page, blueprint, packet, project file, source record, or implementation page described in the document.
public_standard_check.Source use: Sources are used as method and source background, domain grounding, or project history; direct quotation and stronger factual claims require passage-level review.
public_standard_check.Plan: Bind claims to exact source anchors, visible implementation evidence, or a narrower public claim.
public_standard_check.Status: Current support record for the professional package unless explicitly marked archival.
public_standard_check.Remaining Gaps: Passage-level source check, implementation verification, citation cleanup, and proportional public wording remain open where evidence is incomplete.
